reasoning: The Lean formalization faithfully captures the informal Putnam 2018 B1 problem. P is defined as the Finset of vectors (a,b) with 0 ≤ a ≤ 2 and 0 ≤ b ≤ 100. Pvdiff = P \ {v}. The partition conditions Q ∪ R = Pvdiff and Q ∩ R = ∅ correctly encode a disjoint partition. Q.card = R.card captures "equal size", and the two sum equalities on components 0 and 1 correctly capture "equal sum" of vectors (vector sum is componentwise). The biconditional with the solution set is faithful: when v ∉ P, the LHS is false via the v ∈ P conjunct, and the solution set {v | ∃ b, 0 ≤ b ≤ 100, Even b, v = ![1,b]} is automatically contained in P, so the iff behaves correctly in all cases. No hypotheses are dropped or added relative to the informal statement.
